title: Aureline managed-service deletion-job record
description: Boundary schema for the deletion_job_record produced whenever a delete path touches a managed-service copy. The record binds a per-class delete request to a typed deletion_job_state, a non-empty remaining_location_notes set on every non-terminal state, an expected_next_state_change_at RFC 3339 UTC timestamp on every non-terminal state (null on terminal states), and a typed deletion_blocker_class set with inspectable artifact_ref or policy_ref pointers on blocked_* states. Composes over docs/governance/record_state_and_policy_simulation_models.md and the class-level posture in artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml; narrows through the SLO row in artifacts/service/slo_rows.yaml and the retention row in artifacts/service/retention_rows.yaml. Raw payload bytes never cross this boundary. Narrative companion: docs/service/managed_service_seed.md. Adding a deletion_job_state, deletion_blocker_class, managed_copy_kind, remaining_location_kind, or completion_evidence value is additive-minor and bumps schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this record. Additive-minor changes bump this integer.

job_id.description: Stable deletion-job id. Snake_case. MUST NOT contain milestone slugs, raw user names, raw emails, or raw ticket numbers.

record_class_id.description: Record class id resolving to a row in artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml. The class-level posture there is the ceiling; this job narrows through the retention row in artifacts/service/retention_rows.yaml.

managed_copy_kind.description: Which managed-copy kind the job targets. no_managed_copy is valid only on jobs that assert no managed-side action is required (for example a telemetry-descriptor confirmation).

service_surface_refs.description: Service ids from artifacts/service/slo_rows.yaml that this job touches. Every id MUST be admissible under the row's linked_record_class_ids.

acknowledged_at.description: When the managed surface acknowledged the request. Null while the job is still requested_not_acknowledged.

state.description: Typed deletion-job state. Terminal states are completed_all, completed_partial_hold, completed_partial_policy_retention, cancelled_by_user, and cancelled_by_admin.

state_entered_at.description: When the current state was entered. MAY be null while acknowledged_at is null.

expected_next_state_change_at.description: When the job is expected to transition next, RFC 3339 UTC. MUST be non-null on every non-terminal state; MUST be null on terminal completed_* and cancelled_* states. Jobs that cannot honestly predict the next change MUST route through state = unknown_pending_recheck.

remaining_location_notes.description: Structured notes naming where the data may continue to live until the job terminates. MUST be non-empty on every non-terminal state; MAY be empty on completed_all; MUST be non-empty on completed_partial_hold, completed_partial_policy_retention, and every non-terminal state. Raw path bytes, raw URLs, raw credentials, and raw payload bytes do not appear here.

blockers.description: Typed blockers. MUST be non-empty on blocked_* states and on completed_partial_hold / completed_partial_policy_retention; MUST be empty on completed_all, cancelled_by_user, and cancelled_by_admin.

destruction_receipt_refs.description: Destruction-receipt ids emitted by the job so terminal claims cite inspectable proof. MUST be non-empty when completion_evidence resolves to destruction_receipt or destruction_receipt_with_retained_subset on the job's retention row.

notes.description: Optional short operator-reviewable note. MUST NOT carry raw user names, raw emails, raw URLs, raw payload bytes, or milestone slugs.

__fixture__.description: Optional fixture annotation block; never part of the runtime record.

allOf.description: Terminal completed_all, cancelled_by_user, and cancelled_by_admin MUST NOT carry blockers and MUST set expected_next_state_change_at to null.
Partial-completion states MUST carry at least one blocker naming why the job is not completed_all, and MUST set expected_next_state_change_at to null (the job is terminal with a retained subset).
Blocked_* states MUST carry at least one blocker and a non-null expected_next_state_change_at.
Non-terminal in-progress states MUST carry a non-null expected_next_state_change_at and non-empty remaining_location_notes.
blocked_by_hold MUST cite at least one hold-class blocker.
blocked_by_policy_retention MUST cite a retention-minimum or policy-freeze-window blocker.
blocked_by_entitlement_expired MUST cite the matching blocker.
blocked_by_service_unavailable MUST cite a service-unavailable or boundary-recheck blocker.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short product-term sentence suitable for admin explain, support, and offboarding review surfaces. MUST NOT carry raw user names, raw emails, raw URLs, raw path bytes, raw payload bytes, or milestone slugs.

stable_token.description: Snake_case stable token. Does not contain milestone slugs.

lower_snake_id.description: Lower_snake identifier drawn from an upstream vocabulary row (record class id, service id, etc.).

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

managed_copy_kind.description: Managed-copy kind the job targets. Mirrors the managed_copy_kind vocabulary in artifacts/service/retention_rows.yaml.

deletion_blocker_class.description: Closed deletion-blocker vocabulary. A job MAY carry more than one blocker; every blocker MUST cite an artifact_ref or a policy_ref.

artifact_ref.description: Repo-relative or registry-ref pointer to an inspectable artifact that names the blocker (for example a hold-class row, a retention-minimum row, or a policy-bundle decision).

policy_ref.description: Repo-relative or registry-ref pointer to the governing policy row or decision.

expected_clear_at.description: Optional per-blocker prediction of when the blocker clears. Null when the blocker has no honest prediction.

remaining_location_kind.description: Closed storage-surface vocabulary for remaining-location notes. Raw paths and raw URLs never appear; rows cite a storage surface by class.

cleared_by_next_state.description: Whether the next expected state change is expected to clear this location.

retention_row_ref.description: Pointer to the row in artifacts/service/retention_rows.yaml this job narrows.

record_class_registry_ref.description: Pointer to the class-level row in artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml.

record_state_model_ref.description: Optional pointer to docs/governance/record_state_and_policy_simulation_models.md or a specific state-machine section.

slo_row_refs.description: Optional pointers to rows in artifacts/service/slo_rows.yaml for every service_surface_refs entry.

continuity_packet_ref.description: Optional pointer to a local_core_continuity_packet_record (raw file or packet id) explaining the managed-service state at the time of the job.
